description: xrpl account — Wallet aliases, global --node flag, and full account command suite: info, balance, transactions, offers, trust-lines, channels, nfts, set, delete

userStories.title: Extract getKeystoreDir helper + add label field + resolveAccount() utility
userStories.description: As a developer, I need shared keystore utilities (getKeystoreDir, resolveAccount) and a label field on KeystoreFile so wallet aliases work consistently across all commands.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `label?: string` to the `KeystoreFile` type in `src/utils/keystore.ts`
Add `encryptKeystore` optional 5th parameter `label?: string`; include it in output JSON when provided

Extract `getKeystoreDir(options: { keystore?: string }): string` from duplicated command code into `src/utils/keystore.ts`; resolution order: options.keystore → XRPL_KEYSTORE env var → ~/.xrpl/keystore/
Update all wallet commands (import, list, remove, decrypt-keystore, change-password) to import and use `getKeystoreDir` from keystore.ts instead of their own local copy

Add `resolveAccount(addressOrAlias: string, keystoreDir: string): string` to `src/utils/keystore.ts`: if input starts with 'r' and length is 25–34, return unchanged; otherwise scan *.json files in keystoreDir for matching label field and return address; throw 'Error: no wallet with alias <name> found in keystore' if not found
Unit tests in `src/utils/keystore.test.ts`: resolveAccount returns address as-is; resolves a known alias; throws on unknown alias
Existing wallet E2E tests still pass after getKeystoreDir refactor

userStories.title: wallet alias set and wallet alias remove
userStories.description: As a user, I want to assign or remove a human-readable alias on an existing keystore entry so I can reference wallets by name.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `src/commands/wallet/alias.ts` with a `wallet alias` subcommand group registered in `src/commands/wallet/index.ts`
`xrpl wallet alias set <address> <name>` reads <address>.json from keystore, sets label to <name>, writes back atomically (tmp + rename)
`alias set` exits 1 with error if <address>.json not found in keystore
`alias set` exits 1 if <name> is already used by a different address unless --force is passed; scans all *.json files in keystore dir to check uniqueness
`xrpl wallet alias remove <address>` removes the label field from <address>.json; exits 1 if file not found
Both subcommands respect --keystore <dir> / XRPL_KEYSTORE env var via getKeystoreDir
E2E test: import a wallet, alias set, read keystore JSON and assert label field matches, alias remove, read JSON and assert label is absent
E2E test: alias set with a duplicate name exits 1; --force overwrites

userStories.title: wallet alias list — show all aliases
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see all named wallets at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `list` subcommand to the `wallet alias` group in `src/commands/wallet/alias.ts`
`xrpl wallet alias list` scans keystore dir, prints one '<alias>  →  <address>' line per wallet with a label
Wallets without a label are omitted
--json outputs array of { alias, address } objects
Prints '(no aliases set)' when no labelled wallets exist
E2E test: import two wallets with --alias flags, run alias list, assert both name→address pairs appear; import third wallet without alias, assert it does not appear
E2E test: --json output is a valid JSON array with alias and address fields

userStories.title: --alias flag on wallet import
userStories.description: As a user, I want to set an alias at import time without a separate command.
acceptanceCriteria: When --alias is provided, pass label to encryptKeystore so it is stored in the keystore file
Apply same alias uniqueness check as `alias set`: scan keystore dir, exit 1 if name taken, --force overrides
Confirmation message updated to: 'Imported account <address> (alias: <name>) to <filepath>' when alias is given
E2E test: wallet import --alias alice, verify keystore JSON contains "label": "alice", verify wallet alias list shows alice
E2E test: importing with a duplicate alias exits 1; --force succeeds

userStories.title: wallet list shows alias column
userStories.description: As a user, I want wallet list to show aliases alongside addresses so I can see the full picture at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: Update `src/commands/wallet/list.ts` to read label from each keystore JSON file
Human-readable output: '<address>  <alias>' where alias column is blank (not a dash) when unset
--json output changes from string[] to { address: string, alias?: string }[] — omit alias key when not set
E2E test: import wallet with alias, run wallet list, assert output line contains both address and alias
E2E test: --json output contains alias field when set, no alias field when unset
Existing wallet list E2E tests updated to match new output format

userStories.title: Global --node flag on root command + getNodeUrl() utility
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a single --node configuration point on the root command so no subcommand needs to duplicate it.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `.option('-n, --node <url>', 'XRPL node URL or network name (mainnet|testnet|devnet)', process.env.XRPL_NODE ?? 'testnet')` to root program in `src/index.ts`
Create `src/utils/node.ts` exporting `getNodeUrl(cmd: Command): string` that calls `resolveNodeUrl(cmd.optsWithGlobals().node)`
Remove --node option from `src/commands/account.ts` (the existing stub); update its action handler to use `getNodeUrl(cmd)` — action handler must accept Command instance as last argument
E2E test: run `xrpl --node testnet account info <known-testnet-address>` and assert it returns account data (use a well-known funded testnet address)
E2E test: run with `XRPL_NODE=testnet` env var and no --node flag, assert it connects successfully

userStories.title: account info — rewrite and restructure into account/ directory
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see the full on-ledger state of an XRPL account with all relevant fields.
acceptanceCriteria: `xrpl account info <address-or-alias>` uses resolveAccount() for alias resolution with keystoreDir from getKeystoreDir({ keystore: undefined })

Human-readable output prints labelled lines: Address, Balance (converted from drops to XRP), Sequence, Owner Count, Reserve (base + owner × increment), Flags (hex + decoded names via xrpl.parseAccountRootFlags), Domain (hex decoded to UTF-8, omitted if not set), Email Hash (omitted if not set), Transfer Rate (raw integer + '(<rate/1e9> fee factor)', omitted if 0), Tick Size (omitted if 0)
--json outputs the raw account_data object from the XRPL response
Uses getNodeUrl(cmd) — action must receive Command instance as last arg
E2E test: run account info with a well-known testnet address (or fund one with fundFromFaucet), assert output contains 'Address:' and 'Balance:' lines
E2E test: --json output contains Account and Balance fields

userStories.title: account balance — XRP balance shorthand
userStories.description: As a user, I want a quick one-liner to check an account's XRP balance.
acceptanceCriteria: `xrpl account balance <address-or-alias>` fetches account_info and prints '<amount> XRP' (drops divided by 1,000,000)
--drops flag prints raw drops as a plain integer string instead
Uses resolveAccount() for alias and getNodeUrl(cmd) for node
E2E test: fund testnet account, run account balance, assert output matches /^\d+(\.\d+)? XRP$/
E2E test: --drops output is a plain integer string with no 'XRP' suffix

userStories.title: account transactions — list recent transactions
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see an account's recent transactions with key fields visible at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: `xrpl account transactions <address-or-alias>` fetches account_tx with ledger_index_min: -1
--marker <json-string> enables pagination; accepts the marker value from a previous --json response
Human-readable output: one line per tx formatted as '<ledger>  <type>  <result>  <hash>'
Prints '(no transactions)' if the account has no transaction history
E2E test: fund a testnet account, submit a self-payment, run account transactions, assert the transaction hash appears in output

userStories.title: account offers — list open DEX offers
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see all open DEX offers placed by an account.
acceptanceCriteria: Human-readable output: one line per offer — '#<seq>  <taker-pays> → <taker-gets>  quality: <quality>'
XRP amounts displayed as '<value> XRP' (converted from drops); issued tokens as '<value> <currency>/<issuer>'
Prints '(no open offers)' if the account has no offers
E2E test: run account offers on a fresh testnet account, assert output is '(no open offers)'

userStories.title: account trust-lines — list trust lines
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see all trust lines an account has established.
acceptanceCriteria: Human-readable output: one line per line — '<currency>/<account>  balance: <bal>  limit: <limit>'
--peer <address> option filters to trust lines with a specific peer
E2E test: run account trust-lines on a fresh testnet account, assert output is '(no trust lines)'

userStories.title: account channels — list payment channels
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see all payment channels where an account is the source.
acceptanceCriteria: Human-readable output: one line per channel — '<channel-id>  dest: <dest>  amount: <xrp> XRP  balance: <xrp> XRP' (drops converted to XRP)
E2E test: run account channels on a fresh testnet account, assert output is '(no payment channels)'

userStories.title: account nfts — list NFTs owned
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see all NFTs an account owns.
acceptanceCriteria: Human-readable output: one line per NFT — '<nft-id>  taxon: <taxon>  serial: <serial>  flags: <flags>'
E2E test: run account nfts on a fresh testnet account, assert output is '(no NFTs)'

userStories.title: account set — AccountSet transaction
userStories.description: As a user, I want to update my account's on-ledger settings (domain, email hash, transfer rate, flags).
acceptanceCriteria: Key material options (at least one required): --seed <seed>, --mnemonic <phrase>, --account <address-or-alias> (loads from keystore via getKeystoreDir; --password skips interactive prompt with insecure warning to stderr)
Settable fields (at least one required): --domain <utf8-string> (auto hex-encodes), --email-hash <32-byte-hex>, --transfer-rate <integer> (0 or 1000000000–2000000000), --tick-size <n> (0 or 3–15)
Flag options: --set-flag <name> and --clear-flag <name>; accepted names: requireDestTag, requireAuth, disallowXRP, disableMaster, noFreeze, globalFreeze, defaultRipple, depositAuth
--dry-run prints the unsigned tx JSON to stdout without calling client.submit()
E2E test: fund a testnet account, run account set --domain example.com --seed <seed>, then run account info and assert Domain field is present
E2E test: --dry-run prints JSON containing TransactionType: 'AccountSet' and does not submit

userStories.title: account delete — AccountDelete transaction
userStories.description: As a user, I want to delete an account and sweep its XRP to a destination address.
acceptanceCriteria: Key material: --seed <seed>, --mnemonic <phrase>, or --account <address-or-alias> (same pattern as account set)
Prints warning to stderr: 'Warning: AccountDelete is irreversible. The account will be removed from the ledger.'
Without --yes, prompts 'Confirm? [y/N]' interactively; exits without submitting if user enters anything other than 'y' or 'Y'
--dry-run prints unsigned tx JSON without calling client.submit()

E2E test: fund a testnet account (source), fund a destination account, run account delete --destination <dest> --seed <seed> --yes, then run account info on the deleted address and assert it returns an error containing 'actNotFound'
E2E test: --dry-run outputs JSON with TransactionType: 'AccountDelete' and does not submit
